description: A popular cultural and creative park that combines traditional architecture with modern cultural and creative features, making it a must-visit spot.
An important worship site for Mazu belief along China's southeastern coast, featuring ancient architecture and bustling incense offerings.
An ancient Ming Dynasty garrison town, preserving old city walls and traditional streets and alleys, with a tranquil and charming night view.
The ruins of the southern gate of the Song-Yuan ancient city, witnessing the historical changes of Quanzhou.
A modern seaside leisure area in Quanzhou, ideal for strolling, relaxing, and enjoying ocean views.
Showcasing the historical and cultural ties between Fujian and Taiwan, offering insights into the development of cross-strait exchanges.
A coastal area with an extended shoreline and equipped with a seaside boardwalk, making it a high-quality seaside leisure destination near Quanzhou.
The most historically rich ancient street in Quanzhou, featuring well-preserved Minnan architectural styles and a variety of local snacks and delicacies.
Quanzhou's famous commercial pedestrian street, where the night market gathers various local specialty snacks.
A Taoist temple built by the sea, dedicated to Zhenwu Emperor, offering a perfect spot for ocean views and blessings, with beautiful coastal scenery.
